chunks.text: Course: EE 290 | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 290. Advanced Topics in Electrical Engineering Catalog Description: The 290 courses cover current topics of research interest in electrical engineering. The course content may vary from semester to semester. Units: 1-4 Also Offered As: ELENG 290 Prerequisites: Consent of instructor. Formats: Fall: 3.0-15.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 2.0-5.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 2.0-5.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 3.0-9.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 3.0-9.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 2.0-6.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 2.0-6.0 hours of lecture per week Fall: 1.0-3.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 1.0-3.0 hours of lecture per week Spring: 3.0-15.0 hours of lecture per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: No Class Schedule (Spring 2026): EE 290-4 – TuTh 14:00-15:29, Cory 521 – Sosale Shankar Sastry EE 290-9 – MoWe 10:00-10:59, ; Fr 13:00-13:59, – Jaijeet Shankar Roychowdhury EE 290-14 – Fr 14:00-15:59, Cory 521 – Tony Yen Class Notes Math 53 (Multi-variable Calculus); EE 117 (Electromagnetic Fields and Waves) EE 194-16/290-16 – TuTh 09:30-10:59, Cory 521 – Anant Sahai , Jiantao Jiao Class Notes Prerequisites: CS 182/282A Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
